In view of the above-noted deficiencies, we recommended which the Management agreed, through the concerned Office/r, to observe the following courses of actions:
The Municipal Accountant closely coordinate with the MAO and immediately verify, analyze, and reconcile the discrepancy between the book balance and the property records to identify the causes of the errors/deficiencies noted and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts;
The MAO include in the physical inventory of livestock animals the one cow valued at ₱87,000.00, pending the approval of the Request for Relief from Accountability, trace any related documents for the carabaos totaling ₱65,100.00, and submit the report to the Accounting Office to facilitate reconciliation and effect any necessary corrections, if needed; and
The MAO and the Municipal Accountant prepare the WOABS Property and Ledger Cards, respectively, in the prescribed format in accordance with MNGAS to document all the necessary details of breeding stocks as at a given period.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Municipal Accounting and Treasury Offices, to conduct regular reconciliations of the cashbook and accounting records to ensure timely corrections and reconciliation of balances.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to submit the summary reports of total fuels issued in CY 2024 to the Municipal Accounting Office. Moving forward, the GSO is enjoined to ensure the timely submission of these reports to the Accounting Office to facilitate the prompt recognition of the expense in the books of accounts.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the necessary adjustment on the Fuel, Oil, and Lubricants Inventory account and Fuel, Oil, and Lubricants Expenses/Prior Period Error account once the summary reports is received to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Municipal Engineering and Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned offices, to closely coordinate and develop a catch-up plan to fast track the implementation of the intended projects of the financial assistance and the request for realignment of funds, including the remaining unimplemented financial assistance from prior years, as appropriate.
We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Administrator, agreed to follow up on their request to reallocate the received financial assistance totaling ₱7,028,290.03 in order to immediately utilize the funds and begin realizing the benefits of the intended project.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the Due to LGU schedule and adjust the negative balances totaling ₱3,289,795.59 to ensure the fair and accurate presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Implementing Offices agreed to submit a recommendation or report to the Municipal Mayor indicating whether the unused balances are still needed or ready for return to facilitate the proper disposition of the unutilized balances totaling ₱1,564,555.96, either by utilizing the funds or returning the same to the SA or the BTr, whichever is applicable.
We further recommended and the MAO agreed to follow up on their request to reallocate the received financial assistance totaling ₱1,915,130.00 in order to immediately utilize the funds.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the Due to NGA schedule and adjust the negative balances totaling ₱658,836.66 to ensure the fair and accurate presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC agreed to follow-up the immediate submission of the lacking liquidation documents for the balance totaling ₱234,741.46, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, as the Special Disbursing Officer, liquidate the cash advances of ₱300,000.00 for the third quarter and ensure proper liquidation of cash advances for confidential funds within the prescribed period in accordance with the liquidation requirements of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
Since the Municipal Mayor was unable to attend the meeting due to prior commitments, and the Executive Secretary was still on leave due to a recent medical operation, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Administrator stated that they would relay the audit observations to the Municipal Mayor and Executive Secretary and ensure compliance with the audit recommendation.

We recommended and the Local Finance Committee agreed to prepare the quarterly LGSF-SBDP Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation using the prescribed format and ensure it is certified correct by its members.
We further recommended and they agreed to post the said report on the LGU website to comply with the transparency requirements of LBC No. 154.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to submit the remaining invitation letter/s sent to the observer for the Audit Team’s evaluation. Moving forward, the BAC and the Municipal Accountant are enjoined to ensure the completeness of supporting documents when submitting to the Audit Team to avoid issuance of audit suspensions, as appropriate.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Engineer, the MPDC, and other concerned offices including end-user departments, to implement the following measures:

Ensure that proposed development projects for the ensuing year are presented with sufficient detail to remove the need for further legislative authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan and facilitate timely procurement and implementation; and
Effectively plan, monitor, and evaluate development projects to lessen, if not eliminate, realignments, and to expedite the timely procurement and implementation of all identified priority development projects under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that for future/ succeeding budgeting, the Municipal Budget Officer and the GAD Focal Person ensure to comply with the mandated budgetary allocations for GAD of at least 5% of the LGU’s total annual budget for the year pursuant to the requirements of JMC 2013-1.

observations.managementAction: Management is appealing to the auditors to consider this transaction. This was the first time of the BAC to undertake procurement of consultancy Services in the updating of CLUP. During the pre-qualification evaluation, the BAC got confused of the documents presented by the lone bidder. Instead of directly awarding the contract to Mr. Niceforo Yroy himself as the individual consultant, per Section V.7 of Annex H RA 9184, the contract was awarded to Nicelroy Konsult, his company which was new then. The output of the consultant was duly received by the LGU and the Audit Team obtained a copy of the Certification dated May 9, 2019 issued by the then Chairperson of the Provincial Land Use Council (PLUC) that the Municipality had submitted its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) for 2017-2026 integrating Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Change Adaptation (DRR-CCA) for review and evaluation by the PLUC. Until now the LGU still withheld the 15% or ₱86,250.00 of the total contract price in view of this audit deficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take-home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for to their daily needs until the next pay day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a. submit a written explanation as to why claims of the contractors for the mobilization fee/advance payment were paid despite on the non-compliance of the required commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond. b. require the Accounting Department to process the disbursement voucher/ claim on the mobilization fee/ advance payment only upon submission by the contractor of the required commercial bank guarantee or a surety bond as prescribed by 2016 IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management’s practice is to release the mobilization claim awarded to contractor’s upon work accomplishment and checking by the engineering department. For so long this has been the process. The Management promised to correct the said practice. In future payments of mobilization, the LGU will require a commercial bank guarantee or a surety bond.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that to avoid disallowance of ₱5,198,200.08 representing variance, the Municipality deduct from the claim of the contractor and /or demand from the contractor refund of the amount.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC, likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered.
We recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental annual procurement plan (APP)..

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that the head of agency shall prepare and instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements

a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical inventory and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommend that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Tuburan for proper reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of depreciation for depreciable assets.

Direct the Chief Accountant to: 1. Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these accounts. 2. Reconcile the Inventory report with the Property cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality. 3. Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement of all accounts.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long-term debt in the FS, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each custody or administration in compliance with the law.

a. Submit the release and use of the LDRRMF shall be supported by LDRRMFIP (LDRRMFIP) for 2020 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to support the legality of the 2020 DRRM transaction, and b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2021 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2021 transaction.
We recommended that the LCE to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly reports for all on going infrastructure projects in the Municipality.

We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, to submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10) days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in accordance with government rules and regulations, attached is the COA Circular 97-61 complete with sample form of monthly report of fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that to avoid disallowance of ₱5,198,200.08 representing variance, the Municipality deduct from the claim of the contractor and /or demand from the contractor refund of the amount.
observations.managementAction:
Per reply to the Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) received by the audit team on April 23, 2019, the Management explained that the conceptualization of the Proposed New Public Market started way back on the years 2010 to 2012 with a series of upgrading and revisions which turned out to have some unforeseen changes also on the estimates. A fast series of transitions of municipal engineers unable to turnover other important documents made difficulties in consolidating properly important records and necessary supporting documents for this project. The initial concept and structural design of the public market was supposedly to have a mat foundation design in order not to adopt pile foundation. However, plans and concepts of these designs were not properly turned over from the previous to the next municipal engineer. Didn’t know fully of the process and history of the project, the present municipal engineer rely on what was available as of the moment. The available plan that the municipal engineer had was the latest approved plan and still another change and variation might affect the project because of the unavailability of the specified materials reflected on the plan for construction.

Out of the total contract cost of ₱68,453,495.35, the Municipality paid to the contractor, thru progress billing of 58% or ₱39,703,027.30, thus, there was still remaining collectible of ₱28,750,468.05. In the event that there is refundable from the contractor’s cost after considering additive and deductive as a result of changes/variations of the on-going implementation, the Agency assured to withhold out of the contractor’s claim the amount established to be refundable from your end.

prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance
with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate
review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts
in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical
count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of
physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate
inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute
to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable
properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance
with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in
accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which
prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should
ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card
for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the
preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording
in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the
20
current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation
expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE
accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in
the Financial Statements.

locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account

to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and

reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of
depreciation for depreciable assets.

We recommended that Management shall take appropriate actions on the failure of the contractor to complete the project on the targeted completion date.

The Municipal Engineer is enjoined to conduct closer monitoring on the project so that completion thereof could be properly undertaken and/or recommend to the LCE the appropriate action suitable to the situation pursuant to the RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
The Management replied that there were no approved work extensions during the duration of work from July 13, 2017 to November 3, 2017; instead two approved suspension of work was approved due to geotechnical verification of soil condition. The project had proceeded with the assumption of good soil condition having existing structure built on it for a long period of time. However, due to recent water seepage that was discovered during the construction, the contractor requested for verification of the soil condition before proceeding with the construction of foundations and other earthworks. They conducted soil investigation at their own expense. However, a certain period of time was needed for the results of the laboratory tests. In this connection, accomplishment of the project was also affected due to the movement of the completion date.

Another factor that affect the project was the unavailability of specified materials of which we need to recalculate structurally for the nearest economical size for the replacement. One of these items were the roofing purlins. The Engineering Department Head called for a meeting with the contractor and agreed to have a weekly monitoring and catch up coordination meeting to check status and resolved further issues of delays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance
with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate
review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in
the financial statements.

We recommended the Head of the Agency to instruct the Local Treasurer/
Cashier/ Collecting Officer to deposit intact all the collections turned over to them
with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical
count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of
physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate
inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute
to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable
properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance
with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in
accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which
prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should
ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card
for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the
preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording
in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the
current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation
expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE
accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in
the Financial Statements.

We recommend that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to
locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the
maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the
provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division
to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and
Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Tuburan for proper
reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of
depreciation for depreciable assets.

We recommended that the management maintain separate depository
account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall
faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan
(LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to
support the legality of the 2019 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2018 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2018
transaction.

We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer
submit the list of all on – going government projects/programs/activities and those
that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA’s
report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

We recommended the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for the
implementation and enforcement of the provisions of R.A. 9003 within their
respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section 12
and Section 16 of the same Act.

We recommended the agency to establish Local Council for the Protection of
Children composing of chosen responsible members of the community including a
representative from the youth sector, as well as representatives from government and
private agencies concerned with the welfare of children.
Recommendation:
In recognizing the right of children to assistance, including proper care and
nutrition, and special protection from all forms of neglect, abuse, cruelty and
exploitation, and other conditions prejudicial to their development, we recommended
the Municipal Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by
appropriating each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for the
operation of the LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the fund allocated for the
protection of children.

We recommended the following
A. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the
certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July
15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current
year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.
B. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the
Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or
certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes not later than
seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF budget.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory taking of all the properties of the Municipality and to reconcile the results with the accounting records and submit a copy of the report to the audit team. We also recommended that the General Services Officer prepare and maintain the property ledger card for each property acquired including those acquired in prior years.
We recommended that to avoid disallowance of ₱5,198,200.08 representing variance, the Municipality deduct from the claim of the contractor and /or demand from the contractor refund of the amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other officials stated in the COA Circular nos. 2015-008 and 2016-004 comply their duties and responsibilities.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with other Department Heads concerned draw a Journal Entry Voucher duly supported with the required schedules and working papers, to take up the Public Infrastructure accounts in the books of the municipality in their appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to take the adjusting entry on the capitalization of the interest paid on loan including interest paid in CY 2017. Furthermore, the interest to be incurred until the construction is completed shall be added to the cost of the Public Market Building.
We recommended that henceforth, the BAC follow the procedures of Section 13 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in inviting observers to enhance the transparency of the procurement process.
We recommended that the Management submit all the lacking documents stated to support the disbursements to avoid audit disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that the Management utilize the 20% DF only for the purpose expressly prescribed by law. It will be immediately disallowed if the Management will charge it again in the 20% DF its future transactions with same nature above.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Management submit all the lacking documents to avoid disallowance in audit. Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC and the Management conform to the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained.

We recommended that Management shall take appropriate actions on the failure of the contractor to complete the project on the targeted completion date.
The Municipal Engineer is enjoined to conduct closer monitoring on the project so that completion thereof could be properly undertaken and/or recommend to the LCE the appropriate action suitable to the situation pursuant to the RA 9184.

We recommended that Management and Implementing Department concerned submit valid justification why the suspension of work and extension of contract time was granted to the contractor considering that the determination of the original contract time during the conduct of detailed engineering and in the preparation of the contract documents were agreed by the parties before contract perfection. Henceforth, the Head of the Implementing Department should comply all the engineering activities enumerated in Item 2 of Annex A of the 2016 R-IRR of RA 9184 prior to bidding and awarding of contract.

We recommended that Management submit justification why the soil investigation and testing was not first conducted prior to the bidding and awarding of the contract. We further recommended that the Implementing Department comply first all the engineering activities prior to the bidding and awarding of contract in all infrastructure projects to be undertaken by the Local Government Unit, pursuant to Section 17.6 and Annex A of the 2016 R-IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management requires the concerned Sangguniang Bayan members comply with the compulsory membership with the Pag-IBIG Fund and allow the Accounting Section to deduct from their payroll the monthly Pag-IBIG premiums contribution to be remitted to the concerned agency.

We recommended that the LSB present the SEF budget in accordance with Section 5.2.2 to determine which projects are given priority and it should be signed by the Co-Chair and majority of all its members in conformity with Section 5.4 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

Recommendation:

All officials who cause the disbursement of public funds should refund the amount of ₱80,000.00. In appropriating funds for a certain purpose, it should be taken into account that the benefits redound to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: This pertains to the previous administration and the current administration cannot comply the recommendation inasmuch as the persons responsible are no longer connected with the municipal government. Thus, the Municipal Mayor prepared a letter addressed to Atty. Eden T. Rafanan, Regional Director, COA Regional Office VII, Cebu City dated February 14, 2018 requesting to render appropriate action on findings of the Annual Audit Reports under the previous administration which cannot be complied and determine the persons responsible/ liable to comply the requirements. Thus, eliminating accumulation of prior years’ findings which are impossible to comply.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

A prompt and serious review and evaluation of the said accounts should be done in order to determine the causes that have led to their dormant state. Collection letters should be sent to determine collectability of the receivables and the possibility of writing it off per COA regulation before making adjustment on the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The request for write-off of accounts on GSIS & COMELEC Receivables is still on process in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.  On KKK receivables, review shall be made and collection letters shall be sent for those that can be located or alive. Further, the conditions of the occurrence of receivables from GSIS and COMELEC could not be ascertained as this had been in the books for 10 years or more and the  employees/s who may have knowledge on it are no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

All drivers and heavy equipment operators should be required to accomplish the monthly report of fuel consumption and submit the same to the auditor as one of the basis in reviewing the reasonableness of the fuel consumption. The supplier should be directed to prepare the bill in consecutive manner as to the date of the transaction to be realistic and to erase doubts of irregularity.
observations.managementAction: Assigned a staff to prepare the report on fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned officials in the implementation and maintenance of the BB project evaluate the operations thereof and revisit the conditions of the MOA and come up with corrective measures as Compliant Operator of BB project and the ultimate attainment of its objectives.

Moreover, all officials concerned are further enjoined to follow and comply the requirements prescribed under the procurement law and COA rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Already submitted the Status Report prepared by the Acting Municipal Agricultural Officer (MAO).

observations.recommendation: The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for to their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2013 Observation No. 10

The net take home pay falling below ₱4,000.00 is the result of numerous loans being granted to employee and the accountant does not have any signatory role in loan forms; only billing statements are forwarded for payroll deduction and the office is obliged to allow the deduction in payroll upon full consent of the employee to avoid penalties.

observations.recommendation: All officials who cause the disbursement of public funds should refund the amount of ₱80,000.00. In appropriating funds for a certain purpose, it should be taken into account that the benefits redound to its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2008

The Municipal Mayor prepared a letter addressed to Atty. Eden T. Rafanan, Regional Director, COA Regional Office VII, Cebu City dated February 14, 2018 requesting to render appropriate action on findings of the Annual Audit Reports under the previous administration which cannot be complied and determine the persons responsible/ liable to comply the requirements. Thus, eliminating accumulation of prior years’ findings which are impossible to comply.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should not pay any claim presented for payment without complete documentation. The Municipal Accountant must review properly every transaction during the pre-audit process to determine completeness of the transaction and to issue review notes should any discrepancy occur to guide officials concerned and the claimant.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2008

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accountant to locate and submit immediately the disbursement vouchers, together with the required supporting documents for CY2008 and CY2010 totaling ₱3,418,778.42. Otherwise, administrative sanctions should be meted on the erring employee, pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD 1445 which provides for the suspension of payment of salaries until compliance of the directive.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2010 Observation No. 1

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the deficiencies. Stop the practice of paying thru reimbursements since it is not one of the modes of procurement. Procurement should be planned and the Bids and Awards Committee should be directed to carry out their mission conscientiously and knowledgeably. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant should monitor closely the disbursement of funds so that no obligations shall be incurred in excess of what was appropriated.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2010 Observation No. 2

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of processing and paying claims which are not fully supported with complete documentation as required under Section 4.6 of PD 1445. Direct the Accountant to assure that all supporting documents are complete and can fully sustain the transactions so that propriety, validity and legality of said transactions can be ascertained.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2010 Observation No. 3

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accountant to submit and comply with the requirements for complete documentation of the transactions as listed on the schedule. Stop the practice of procurement thru reimbursement as it is not one of the modes of procurement as provided in RA9184. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to exercise their functions conscientiously so as to attain the purpose for its creation.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2010 Observation No. 5

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer being the procurement officer of the agency should observe strictly the provisions of Title VI, Book II of the Local Government Code and Section 10 and 48 of RA9184 in the procurement of supplies and materials. Procurement process must be initiated by the department needing the supplies through the preparation of a written requisition duly certified as to its necessity and the specific activity or project for which the supplies shall be used. Likewise, procurement must be given widest publicity in order to obtain prices most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2009 / AAR 2008 Observations No. 2 and No. 3

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the suppliers duly accomplished dealer’s invoice in all its deliveries of supplies and materials with the local government unit for the designated inspector and the person receiving the supplies to match with the Purchase Order to verify whether the delivery coincide with what were requested. Upon payment demand an official receipt as proof of receipt of payment.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2009 Observation No. 4

Same above submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that  the  head  of  agency  shall  instruct  the
Accountant to    prepare  the  Bank  Reconciliation  Statement  (BRS)  on  a
monthly basis  in        accordance  with  COA  Circular  No.  96-011  dated
October 2, 1996 for auditor’s     immediate  review and verification and  to
facilitate    the    immediate    preparation    of         the    necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or  errors  or      other
reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct
balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the Head of the  Agency  to  instruct  the  Local
Treasurer/ Cashier/ Collecting  Officer  to  deposit  intact  all  the
collections turned over to them with the  authorized  depository  bank
daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended the Management to:


     a. Direct Inventory and Disposal Committee of the Municipal Government
        to conduct  a  complete  physical  count  inventory,  annually,  as
        required; Management should treat the matter of physical count  and
        appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern.  A  separate  inventory
        report shall  be  prepared  for  unserviceable  PPE  which  do  not
        contribute  to  the  economic   activity   of   the   Municipality.
        Derecognize unserviceable properties in the  books  and  facilitate
        disposal of these properties in accordance with Section  79  of  PD
        No. 1445.


     b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards  (PPELC)
        in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS  for  LGUs,  Volume  II
        which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.


     c. The  Municipal  Accountant  and  Other  Officers  concerned  should
        provide  for  the  depreciation  on  the  depreciable  assets.  The
        Property Supply Officer should ensure that the  Property  Custodian
        keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE  account  to
        readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation  of
        the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of  recording
        in the books of  accounts  the  appropriate  depreciation  expenses
        pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its
        appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are  necessary  in
        order to present fairly the PPE  accounts  with  its  corresponding
        Accumulated  Depreciation  Expense  Accounts   in   the   Financial
        Statements.

We  recommended  the  Municipal  Accountant,  to  reconcile  the
      reciprocal account in the trust fund maintained by  the  Municipality,
      by identifying  the  causes  of  discrepancies  in  the  corresponding
      subsidiary Ledgers and  to  effect  immediate  proper  and  verifiable
      adjustments   in   the   books   of   accounts.   Likewise,   periodic
      reconciliation of the accounts should be made, to ensure ate  accuracy
      of the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that the management maintain separate  depository
      account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance
      with the law.

We recommended that at  the  end  of  each  year  the  Municipal
      Accountant  and   the   MDRRM   officer   shall   transfer   all   the
      unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to  the
      special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM”.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and  the  MDRRM  officer
      shall faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation  concerning  DRRM
      fund as follows:

            a. Submit the annual local disaster  risk  reduction  management
               fund investment  plan (LDRRMFIP)  for  2018  DRRM  transaction
               per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to support the  legality  of  the
               2018 DRRM  transaction, and


            b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2017 to  avoid  suspending  the  DRRM
               2017 transaction.

We  recommended  the  Municipal  Government   being   primarily
      responsible for the implementation and enforcement of  the  provisions
      of R.A. 9003 within their respective jurisdictions to comply with  the
      laws and regulation stated in Section 12 and Section 16  of  the  same
      Act.

We recommended the agency to establish  Local  Council  for  the
      Protection of Children composing of chosen responsible members of  the
      community including a representative from the youth sector, as well as
      representatives from government and private  agencies  concerned  with
      the welfare of children.

In recognizing the right of children  to  assistance,  including
      proper care and nutrition, and special protection from  all  forms  of
      neglect,  abuse,  cruelty  and  exploitation,  and  other   conditions
      prejudicial  to  their  development,  we  recommended  the   Municipal
      Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by  appropriating
      each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for  the
      operation of the LCPC and to maximize  the  utilization  of  the  fund
      allocated for the protection of children.

We recommended the agency to appropriate in its annual budget no
      less than twenty percent (20%)  of  its  annual  IRA  for  development
      projects in compliance of Section 287 of  Republic  Act  7160  or  the
      Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended the agency  to  comply  with  the  provision  and
      guidelines stipulated in the DILG - DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No.
      2017 - 001 dated February 22, 2017. The appropriation and  utilization
      of the twenty percent (20%) Development fund shall contribute  to  the
      attainment  of  desirable  socio-economic  development  &  environment
      management outcomes of  the  LGU  and  shall  partake  the  nature  of
      investment or capital expenditure”

We recommended the following

     A. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to  the  LSB
        of the certified  statement  containing  the  following  enumerated
        below, not later than July 15 of the current year as basis for  the
        formulation of SEF budget.


            I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
           II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the  current
               year;
          III. The estimated SEF income for the last  two  quarters  of  the
               current year; and
           IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.


     B.  The  LSB  shall  furnish  the  Local  Budget  Officer,  the  Local
        Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of  the  SEF  Budget  as
        basis for disbursement or certification of  availability  of  funds
        and for recording purposes not later than seven (7) days after  the
        approval of the SEF budget.

We recommended the Audited agency to appropriate 5% of the total
      budget appropriation of the agency and shall  submit  a  copy  of  the
      Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the COA Audit Team assigned to the
      agency within five (5) working days from the received of the  approved
      plan from the PCW or their mother or central offices. Likewise, a copy
      of the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished the said
      Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the
      preceding year.

Direct the persons responsible to settle  the  audit  suspension
      through compliance with the requirements indicated in  the  Notice  of
      Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must
      settle the same in accordance with COA  Circular  No.  2009-006  dated
      September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules  and  Regulations  on
      the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular  No.  2012-001  dated
      June  14,  2012.  Re:  Revised  Documentary  Requirements  for  Common
      Government Transaction must be properly observed.

We reiterate our recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory taking of all the properties of
the Municipality and to reconcile the results with the accounting records and submit a
copy of the report to the audit team.

a.) Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate with the
Municipal School Board (MSB) and communicate with DepEd Tuburan
District Office and request for the immediate liquidation of outstanding
cash advances granted to DepEd officials and employees;
b.) Require the MSB to authorize the municipal treasurer to disburse
funds from the SEF pursuant to the budget prepared and in accordance
with existing rules and regulations; and
c.) Recommend for write-off all unsettled cash advances which had been in
the books for over ten (10) years in accordance with the guidelines set
forth under COA Circular 2016-005 on the Guidelines and Procedures on
the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash
Advances and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies
(NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and
Controlled Corporations (GOCCs).

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat prepare the APP using the prescribed
form based on all the Project Procurement Management Plans included in the
budget proposals and indicate all the required information therein as enumerated
in Section 7.3.4 of the 2016 IRR of R.A. 9184.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor send the BAC Chairperson,
Members of the BAC and Secretariat including the members of the Technical
Working Group to training/s on procurement to professionalize its procurement
officials in order to promote and attain good governance by adhering to the
principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its
procurement process.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee submit the lacking documents indicated in the attached schedule to avoid
disallowance in audit. Henceforth, we recommend that the BAC follow strictly the
documentary requirements pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

a. Submit the stated documents not attached to the contract; and
b. Require the Municipal Engineer Designate to prepare the PERT/CPM and
detailed computation of the contract time and present to the Municipal
Mayor for approval and submit copy of these documents to the audit team.

We recommend that Management should ensure to indicate the delivery terms
in the PO, dates of delivery in the Sales/Charged Invoices, dates of inspection and
acceptance in the IAR and require the supplier/contractor to document deliveries thru
the issuance of DR to not preclude the government in imposing liquidated damages for
any breach thereof.

We recommend that the Municipality require the Municipal Budget Officer to
provide the necessary appropriation representing one percent of the IRA for programs,
projects and activities for the protection of children.

Furthermore, the Municipal Mayor as the MCPC Chairman shall prepare
programs, projects and activities in accordance to R.A. 9344.

We recommend that the Management require the BAC to cause the submission
of the lacking documents to avoid disallowance in audit. Henceforth, the BAC shall
automatic declare disqualification of any bidder who fails to submit the eligibility and
technical documentary requirements of any public bidding conducted.

We recommend that the Municipal Local Executive as well as the Legislative
officer should fill up the vacant positions and should provide appropriation for a more
effective administration of those offices and to deliver the basic services needed by the
constituents. Likewise, the Budget Officer Designate should allocate budget for those
vacant positions to protect collapsing of Plantilla position.

Moreover, the Head of Personnel/Human Resource Management Officer-
Designate should publish the vacancy and if there will be no qualified applicant,
recommend to the Local Chief Executive for the temporary appointment of those
willing and available applicant for the position.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the department concerned
to implement the program in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement so that
the targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the benefits therefrom.

We recommend that the Management (a) stop the granting of cash advances to
the Social Welfare Officer for the payment of food stuffs; (b) require payment of food
items by check directly payable to creditors to whom the money is due; (c) required the
Municipal Accountant to withhold the prescribed creditable value-added tax before
making any payment to seller of goods and services; (d) require the BAC to stop
splitting of the Purchase Request into smaller quantities; (e) require the General
Services Officer to issue Purchase Orders for all purchases of food items; and (f)
require the BAC to adapt appropriate mode of procurement.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
withhold all creditable taxes upon payment of goods and services to the suppliers in
order to assure additional income for the government.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Municipal
Officials to settle their accountabilities with the Accounting Section within fifteen (15)
days through the submission of the necessary supporting documents and to refund any
excess amount or submit a written explanation for failure to do so.

We recommend that the statutory/mandatory positions required under the
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April
4, 2014 be made and filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly
institutionalize the organization of the LDRRMO and have continuity in service and the
implementation of its programs.

We recommend that Management direct the official concerned to prepare and
submit a Revised Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2017 in
prescribed format, certified correct by the Municipal Accountant through the
LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor of the municipality. Henceforth, the monthly
report shall be prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor on or before the
15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that Management:
1. Direct the Secretary to the SB and Human Resource Management Officer to
furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of SB Resolution, Contracts and
certifications relating to the hiring of job orders as provided in Section 77 of
the Local Government Code and Item 4.1.13 of COA Circular 2012-001.
2. Require the Job Order personnel to submit quantified accomplishment
report to form part of the supporting papers of their wages.
3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to require the
personnel concerned to submit the documentary requirements before claims
are to be paid.

We recommend that the current Local School Board revisit the provisions of the
Section 98 (b) and (c) of R.A. 7160 and reorganize the composition of the Municipal
School Board.

We recommend that the SEF budget year for CY 2018 be prepared and
approved by the Municipal School Board (MSB) in accordance with Sections 5.2.2 and
5.4 of DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer ensure that the detailed estimates
for each work item supports the summary of detailed breakdown of the ABC.
Maximize the application of the LGU’s power to levy real property tax pursuant to Sections 232 and 233, Chapter 4 of the Local Government Code or R.A. 7160.

Impose Special Levy on Real Property in accordance with Section 235 of Chapter 5, R.A. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991 which should be 1% on the assessed value of real property the same as the basic tax

The accountable officers/collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set more effective control measures to avoid or minimized delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action on concerned accountable employees under her/his supervision, if necessary.

Instruct the HRMO - designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and 
Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contract of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, Specific instructions  given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which  should be likewise attached to the contract

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular that only allowable expenses are charges to the fund; and
Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submits same to the Office of the Auditor.

The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability=DRRM” (Code 438).
Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) must be maintained.
The Municipal Budget Officer maintains record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to promptly submit the required Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities.
  
Likewise, we also recommend that the details required in the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities should be properly filled-in.

Management shall prioritize the implementation of the programs on solid waste and do such other acts required from LGU pursuant to Republic Act 9003 particularly on waste segregation and collection until its disposal to the dumpsite to protect the public from possible health hazard.

We recommended that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative its entirety.

We recommended that the Local School Board should submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees.

We also requesting the Accountant   you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record. (See Annex-A   for Schedule )
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period. (See Annex-C for schedule)
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875. (See Annex-D for schedule)

Properly  Observe COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 the prescribed  deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Management should appropriate at least five percent (5%) of the total budget for GAD programs/activities/projects, prepare the required Annual GAD Plan and budget as well as the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report pursuant to Section 2.3 of Joint Circular No.

Submit to the auditor a list of basic services and facilities worth P16,100,000.00 representing the 20% Development Fund for inspection as to the existence and extent of accomplishment and for evaluation as well whether such facilities are effective and had been undertaken in an efficient and economic manner.

Also, we recommend submit to our office the monthly report on Program/Projects and Activities (PPAs).

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.

We recommend that management:
1. Review the utilization of the 20% development fund and realign those that are not development related items to the general fund proper; to the existing MOA executed by and between LGU-Tuburan and DSWD, RO 7, Cebu City
2. Adhere to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 4

Wages for KALAHI- CIDDS’ 2017 20% development projects and other administrative expenses were already charged to the General Fund proper.

Counterpart for the projects undertaken by LGU-Tuburan and DSWD, RO 7, Cebu City

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
1. Review the utilization of the 20% development fund and realign those that are not development related items to the general fund proper; and
2. Adhere to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 5

For CY 2017, the expenses of fuel for clean and green/garbage collections are within the PAPs covered under Section 4 of DILG-DBM Joint Circular 2011-1. While office supplies and materials (this is pertaining to KALAHI-CIDDS') are already corrected and charged to the appropriate account as in the General Fund as stated in item No. 4 above.

Likewise, expenses for maintenance of water system of ₱520,710.00 were also corrected and charged to the General Fund.

Further, Tuburan Water Work System is now operated under Joint Venture Agreement (JVA-PPP) thru SAVS Tuburan Water Work System effective April 1, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant Designate should strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

The Municipal Mayor and the Local Finance Committee are enjoined to include in the preparation of the Annual/Supplemental Budget an appropriation for the maintenance of the water system to sustain its operations.

The Municipal Budget Officer is also enjoined to stop charging the maintenance of water system operations to the 20% Development Fund and to observe strictly the limitations on the utilization provided in DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 6

The Management did not charged to the 20% development fund and not recorded as Construction in Progress-Infra Assets for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that appropriations for MOOE for the Office of the MDRRM be included in the annual budget of the local government to provide sufficient funds for the DRRM programs.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 9

The MDRRMO strictly adhere to the rules and regulation and carefully classified expenses in the implementation of program/project under his office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Local School Board diligently comply with the provisions of Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG. Expenses not within the purposes of the Special Education Fund (SEF) should be charged to the appropriations of the general fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 10

The Local School Board is now very careful in the utilization/disbursement of Special Education Fund. Further, during the year the Municipal Budget Officer appropriated budget Aid to Education.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the function on revenue collection should be assigned to permanent employee and properly bonded with the Bureau of the Treasury, to protect the funds of the municipality. Refrain from designating Job Order personnel in the revenue collection. Further, we also recommend that the Management review the existing personnel complement of their different offices and the actual functions of employees. There could be offices that have excess personnel, thus, the team recommended that these employees be reassigned to the Treasury Department to complement their manpower.

The Municipal Treasurer is enjoined to install a check and balance on the treasury functions among the office staff and give more close monitoring on the collection function.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 11

The Municipal Mayor issued the Memorandum reassigning Mr. Salberto Mercado and Rodrigo Montebon, both regular employees, from the Treasury Department certified by the Municipal Treasurer, there were still revenue collectors that were not bonded because they were Job Orders.

In addition, Ms. Luisa Dumangon and Ms. Estrella Bayo was transferred from MSWDO and MPDO on September 12, 2017 and January 29, 2018 respectively to MTO, aside for the hiring of Ms. Diana Largo as regular employee under MTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management diligently observe the provisions of pertinent law, rules and regulations on the grant of cash advances to protect the fund of the Municipal Government. In addition, any amount in excess of the cash advance should be refunded immediately by the Disbursing Officer concerned to the Municipal Treasurer thru an issuance of Official Receipt and deposited back to the treasury account of the municipality, to complete the liquidation of a particular cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016 Observation No. 12

The Municipal Accountant strictly observed the rules and regulations in the granting of cash advance to the Disbursing Officer (DO). No cash advance shall be granted unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated.
