The Municipal Treasurer is enjoined to install a check and balance on the treasury functions among the office staff and give more close monitoring on the collection function.

We recommend that the Management diligently observe the provisions of pertinent law, rules and regulations on the grant of cash advances to protect the fund of the Municipal Government.

We further recommend that all cash advances for personal services should be equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period and that any amount in excess of the cash advance should be refunded immediately by the Disbursing Officer concerned to the Municipal Treasurer thru an issuance of Official Receipt and deposited back to the treasury account of the municipality, to complete the liquidation of a particular cash advances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Department Heads to prepare their Project Procurement Management Program (PPMP) for consolidation by the BAC Secretariat into an Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

The Budget Officer and BAC Secretariat are further enjoined to ensure that the APP is linked to the Annual/Supplemental Budget.

Any changes of the PPMPs and APP during the year shall be undertaken in accordance with Section 7.4 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, as amended.

We recommend that the agency submit copies of the contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days from issuance as mandated under COA Circular No. 2009-001. Together with the bidding, BAC resolution minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875.

a) The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438). b) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) be maintained. c) The Municipal Budget Officer maintain record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.

The Subproject Manager/Engineer Designate should conduct inspection of all the subprojects implemented by the recipient barangays to validate the existence and accomplishment of these projects and to furnish the team with copies of Monthly Physical and Financial Accomplishment Report submitted to the DILG for further assessment.

We recommend that management should revisit the Memorandum of Agreement and comply with all requirements stipulated therein.
observations.managementAction: One of the provisions of DILG guidelines in the implementation of RAY Projects with the LGU states that when a local government unit (of which a barangay is one) has the capability to implement the project by administration may be given the responsibility to do so. Given this provision, the undersigned released the funds to the respective barangays for them to implement in accordance with the MOA inasmuch as the projects were labor intensive and they have direct link to its constituents in organizing labor force for the rehabilitation of facilities. Further, an Executive Order No. 5 Series of 2015 was created for the Designation of Subproject Managers, of which the Barangay Captain themselves were designated for the Implementation of Recovery Assistance on Yolanda (RAY) Batch 2. It further states that the Barangay Captains shall oversee the subproject implementation with the help and close monitoring of the Municipal Engineer. As a result, attached are the Inspection Reports and Certificates of Completion issued by the Barangay Captain, MPDC and Municipal Engineer that the projects have been satisfactorily completed.    Also, the office of the DILG conducted inspection of these projects and declared completed per Program of Work. Although there were lapses in the documentation in the procurement process per Notice of Suspensions issued as a result of the post audit conducted, the same are on process of compliance by the barangay treasurer and the BRK.
observations.reason: In compliance with the Notices of Suspension issued by the audit team, the Barangay officials concerned had submitted the documentary requirements on the covered transactions (re: repair of brgy facilities implemented by barangays).  Some of the documents required were inspection reports issued by the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of inspection on the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly follow and comply with the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and ascertain that all supporting documents as enumerated are attached to the paid payrolls/DVs.
observations.managementAction: We are currently using Biometric timekeeping facility wherein the management have electronic record of work attendance of the employees. Further, for the Job Order employees, the HRMO staff prepared the payrolls based on the DTRs submitted by the employees, however, due to its bulk, the   Disbursing Officer submitted the supporting documents separately. Now, the DO  attached to the payrolls the complete supporting documents upon liquidation of cash advances and submitted to the Accountant in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445
observations.reason: Verification on some payroll, it was observed that payroll were already attached with Daily Time Record of Laborers and Job order personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management transfer its time deposit maintained at UCPB Savings Bank – Tuburan Branch to the Land Bank of the Philippines or Development Bank of the Philippines, to eliminate exposure of government funds from certain risks particularly on possible insolvency and irregularities of private banks, pursuant to Department of Finance (DOF) Department Order No. 27-05.
observations.managementAction: Terminated the Time Deposit with the UCPB Savings Bank in March 2016.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer had already terminated the Time Deposit maintained at the UCPB Savings Bank.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer require the MSWDO to prepare the PPMPs to be included in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as prescribed under the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We also recommend that the BAC conduct public bidding to obtain more participating bidders/suppliers and avail of discounts inherent in bulk purchasing, to obtain the most advantageous price for the government.
observations.managementAction: Financial Assistance downloaded from DSWD, FO 7 for the Supplementary Feeding Program for CY 2016 are already included in the PPMP of MSWDO and likewise consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan. Further, the BAC conducted public bidding in the implementation of the program twice but failed because there was no interested bidder participated in the bidding hence, the BAC decided to procure thru Negotiated Procurement. A photocopy of Schedule of Public Bidding for the month of November 2016 was submitted of which the public bidding of Supplementary Feeding was included.
observations.reason: Food stuffs for the supplementary feeding was public bidded in CY 2016 but was a failure after two publications in the PHILGEPS.  The BAC proceed with the negotiated procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management procure common-use supplies directly from the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM) to comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 and Section 53.5 of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: During the year the municipality procured common-use supplies thru PS-DBM procurement in the first and second semesters. Supplies not available in the PS-DBM are the ones procured in the market thru shopping.
observations.reason: The supplies not available in the PS-DBM were procured thru small value procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management refrain from indicating the brand names of items to be purchased in the purchase request and canvass forms pursuant to Section 18 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 to prevent illegal expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The BAC strictly scrutinized all Purchase Requests submitted to GSO in order to ensure that specifications for the procurement of goods is based on relevant characteristics, functionality and performance requirements. Those with brand names  are returned to end-users for appropriate adjustments except those stated in Section 18        of the  2016  revised IRR which states that “Specifications for the procurement of Goods shall be based on relevant characteristics, functionality and/ or performance requirements. Reference to brand names shall not be allowed except for items or parts that are compatible with the existing fleet or equipment of the same make and brand, and to maintain the performance, functionality and useful life of the equipment.”
observations.reason: The management was already aware of this ruling and had stopped indicating the brand name of the items in the Purchase Requests.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit the required authority from the SB for the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract in behalf of the LGU to implement the projects totaling P457,994.56.
observations.managementAction: All projects implemented during the year under Aid to SB Priority Development Projects and 20% Development Fund were supported with authority from SB thru SB Resolutions. However, in compliance with your recommendation the SB issued Resolution No. 2016-53 dated June 6, 2016 to support that all grants and financial assistance released without prior authorization were deemed approved.
observations.reason:
Prior year transactions pertaining to the audit observations was rectified through the issuance of the SB Resolution 2016-53 dated June 6, 2016 approving all financial assistance released without authorization were deemed approved. 

Current release as financial assistance to brgy projects were supported with SB resolution approving the aid to the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant - Designate shall deduct the equivalent amount of the absences incurred on regular sessions from the RATA of the concerned elective officials in conformity with Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated on May 15, 2013.
observations.managementAction:
The concern SB members were required to  refund the equivalent amount of their  absences incurred on regular sessions for the year 2014 and 2015 in the total amount of P198,964.39 as shown below:
2014      116,089.39
2015        79,687.50
OR No.
115537713       
                 3,187.50
Total     198,964.39

Also, as an internal control measures, and in order to prevent recurring of the same audit deficiency, it was agreed that payroll for RATA shall be paid on the first week of the following month. SB payroll shall be reviewed and checked by the SB Secretary for confirmation of attendance in the log sheet while for the department heads, it shall be checked and reviewed by the HRMO prior to processing in the Accounting Section.
observations.reason: Traced to Official Receipt and found in order.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant - Designate draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the correcting entries, debiting Other Receivables for P40,000.00 and crediting Prior Year Adjustments.

The MSWDO is also enjoined to submit immediately the required documents in compliance with the Notice of Suspension, so that the audit team can complete the evaluation of the program.
observations.managementAction: In compliance with the recommendation, the Municipal Accountant already drawn JEV No. 100-164-1858 dated April 18, 2016 adjusted the expense account from DONATION to OTHER RECEIVABLES.
observations.reason: Traced to JEV No. 100-164-1858 dated April 18, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) should prepare the GAD accomplishment report in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-001 so that proper monitoring and performance evaluation of GAD related projects/programs and activities can be made.
observations.managementAction: GAD accomplishment report for CY 2015 which was prepared by the previous Chairperson, GFPS Secretariat, MSDWO, Ms. Berlina A. Cana was already submitted.
observations.reason: Had submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report for Cy 2015 and 2016.

observations.recommendation:
With the Supreme Court’s declaration of unconstitutionality of the PDAF and CDF, disbursements and utilization of the funds are now deemed irregular, improper and/or unlawful. Hence, by virtue of this declaration, government officials and/or private individuals who authorized the disbursements/ utilization of the PDAF may be held responsible for the violation thereof.

We therefore recommend that the unspent PDAF funds be returned to the National Treasury so that it can be used for the other priority projects of the government.
observations.managementAction: Already returned to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: Traced to Check and Deposit slips and found in order.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) as well as those Focal Persons assigned in other programs of the municipality to observe and adhere strictly the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement for the various programs/projects/ activities.

Further, the heads of the  implementing offices/departments as well the BAC Chairman are enjoined to revisit the Annual Investment Plan and the Annual Procurement Plan to ascertained and determined the proper mode of procurement of various PPAs for  the ensuing year, to avoid committing the same mistakes in the future.
observations.managementAction: During the current year, the BAC submitted the procurement of the services of the Choreographer thru Competitive Public Bidding twice, but failed. So the BAC procured the services thru Negotiated Procurement.
observations.reason: The management had invited to observe the bid opening for the procurement of the services of Choreographer.

observations.managementAction: The 20% Development Fund balances of the prior years were reviewed and realigned to support and fund the priority projects of the administration as identified during the conduct of ELA 2017-2019 such as Improvement of Roads and Construction of Evacuation Centers. Towards the end of the year 2016, a total of P12,649,617.00 was allocated to the construction of evacuation centers in various barangays and are now on going. The Improvement of Roads specifically the concreting of municipal roads are now on bidding process totaling 8M. Further, preparation of POW and Detailed Engineering Designs are now on-going on items under the 20% DF.
observations.reason: Had utilized substantial amount from the appropriation of the 20% DF in the implementation of priority projects in CY 2016.  It had implemented/starts the construction of evacuation centers in various barangays, construction/improvement of various waterworks systems and concreting of barangay roads.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management should design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the weak internal control on the management of the operation of waterworks. Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of the economic enterprise in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income.

Furthermore, the Municipal Treasurer is enjoined to adopt an enhance collection system from the water consumers and conduct an extensive collection of accounts receivable.
observations.managementAction: Operation of Waterworks for CY 2015 collected in the total amount of P3,481,163.87 while total appropriation was P2,494,410.00 or an excess of P986,763.87 over target. On the other hand, for                     CY 2016, total collections was P3,262,382.62 against the appropriation of P2,322,314.00 or an excess of P940,068.62. Although there are appropriations in the 20% DF for water projects, these were spent for the on-going rehabilitation and expansion inasmuch as the collections generated is sufficient only for the daily operating expenses. Further, for the year 2017, the waterworks shall be operated by SAVS Waterworks thru PPP-JVA.

observations.recommendation: The Accountant to cause the reversal of the entries made on the fully depreciated PPE items that are considered serviceable property items in order to reflect the correct balances of the respective PPE and Other Assets accounts.
observations.managementAction: Effective CY 2015 procurement of medicines/medical supplies were charged to Inventory Account. The Municipal Accountant would only draw JEV upon submission of RIS in order to book up the utilization of medicines to corresponding expense account and thereby reducing the inventory account in the FS.
observations.reason: Recorded the purchases of supplies and medicines under inventory accounts and recorded the expenses upon submission from different department the report of its issuances.

observations.recommendation: Management should adhere strictly to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payments for government transactions to be made in check, and payable directly to the creditor, or to a Disbursing Officer as a cash advance, to be liquidated.  Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials who earlier paid the transaction in cash.  Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash, through the cash advance issued to them.
observations.managementAction: It may be noticed that in our current transactions we do not tolerate payment on reimbursement basis, however, there were really situation of which reimbursement could not be avoided and was extremely necessary in the operation of the agency but limited to the Municipal Mayor only.
observations.reason: Reimbursement on expenses had been minimized

observations.recommendation: The municipal accountant should transfer the value of properties recorded in the Trust Fund to the General Fund Books so that these can be subjected to depreciation in accordance with Section 4 par. O, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. A thorough verification of these accounts should be done by her and she should determine whether these assets are serviceable or not then make the necessary steps.
observations.managementAction:
All balances of properties in the Trust Fund and completed infrastructure projects have been transferred to the General Fund and adjustments for depreciation have been recorded. 
Only projects which are still in progress remain in the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Traced to Financial Statements and found in order.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer majority of their UCPB deposits to the Land Bank of the Philippines, Cebu City Branch and have only a maintaining balance enough for a one month salaries and wages of the officials and employees of the LGU.  In addition, submit authority from the Sanggunian and an authority from the Bangko Sentral Ng Pilipinas relative to the use of UCPB as one of the LGUs depository banks.
observations.managementAction: Terminated the Time Deposit with the UCPB Savings Bank in March 2016.
observations.reason: Terminated the time deposit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should analyze those reconciling items and take up in the books the unrecorded deposits, fund transfer, double and erroneous recorded deposits and other reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: adjusted the reconciling items in the books per JEV No. 100-2016-11-6661          dated  Nov. 15, 2016, except for the amount of P600,000.00 in the  Trust Fund account which were credited to the  bank  on October 18 and November 8, 2016 in the amount of P100,000.00 and P500,000.00 respectively. We have communicated the bank under letter dated January 31, 2017, seeking assistance to identify the sources or the government agency/ies downloaded the funds for appropriate recording in the books.Recorded/Adjusted in the books except the amount of P600,000.00 under Trust Fund account of which the sources could not be identified.
observations.reason:
Had
Verified the adjustment and found in order.

observations.managementAction: Submitted herewith is copy of Actual Inventory Report conducted on PPE. Likewise a copy of summary of all unserviceable properties identified during the conduct of physical inventory. We are now on the process of reconciliation of the balances between the books and the report of actual inventory. Once the reconciling items shall be identified, we will proceed the process of disposal of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason:
The Management still did not come up with a Report of Physical Count of PPE.  Recently, a new GSO-Designate was assigned in the GSO, formerly assigned in the Accounting office .
This observation remained not implemented and reiterated in Part of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board should strictly comply with the provisions of Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG. Expenses not within the purposes of the Special Education Fund (SEF) should be charged to the appropriations of the general fund.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board is now very careful in the utilization/ disbursement of Special Education Fund. Further, during the year the Municipal Budget Officer appropriated budget Aid to Education in the General Fund so   that expenses not within the purposes of SEF which is necessary should be charged to the appropriations in the General Fund.
observations.reason:
Although the management were already aware of these observation, still there were expense items disbursed from SEF in CY 2016, which should be charged thereto.
The Management was advised to get a copy on the new guidelines for CY 2017 on the utilization of SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of the P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise, all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable.

Henceforth, in payment of personnel incentives and similar allowances strict compliance with the provisions of applicable Executive Order as may be implemented by pertinent Budget Circular and other laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: During the year, the municipality released PEI at P5,000.00 each only pursuant to Budget Circular No. 2013-3 dated November 21, 2013. Relative to the excess PEI of P5,000.00 each released in CY 2013 which was issued with the Notice of Disallowance, the refund was held in  abeyance pending the result of the appeal filed with the Commission on Audit. However, those that retired were made to refund and were held in trust fund account.
observations.reason: Waiting for the result of the appeal with the Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for to their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant failed to fully implement the net take home pay to not below P4,000.00 inasmuch as the employees themselves signed in the loan applications to allow the deduction in their payroll even below the amount of P4,000.00.
observations.reason: Net take home pay falling below statutory balance of P4,000 is result of numerous loans being granted to an employee and the accountant does not have any signatory role in loan forms; only billing statements are forwarded for payroll deduction and the office is obliged to deduct from the salary upon full consent of the employee to avoid penalties.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should immediately reconcile the year-end balances of their respective cash in vault accounts. Thereafter, the reconciliation should be made regularly, if possible monthly, so that discrepancies can immediately be detected, and adjustments, if any, can be promptly effected in the books.
observations.managementAction: As of CY 2014 we have made the reconciliation on the account cash in vault between the   cash book and ledger balances. We are working to continue the reconciliation up to the current year.
observations.reason: The reconciliation done in CY 2014 was not continued and currently working to continue the process up to the current year.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the complete list of RPT and SET receivable balances to the Municipal Accountant at year end since CY 2015 because we are using I-TAX system in the operation of RPT.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor did not provide the Municipal Accountant with the required Real Property Assessment Roll.   Subsequently, the Municipal Treasurer also was not able to prepare the certified list of taxpayers showing the names, amount due and collectible for the taxpayers. With the I-TAX program, the Municipal Assessor, Treasurer and Accountant can now prepare and record the RPT and SET Receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers as required with the NGAS.
